resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def subarrayGCD(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        from math import gcd
        n = len(nums)
        res = 0
        for i in range(n):
            g = 0
            for j in range(i, n):
                g = gcd(g, nums[j])
                if g == k:
                    res += 1
                elif g < k:
                    break
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2 log M) where M is the maximum value in nums
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Iterate over every starting index and extend the subarray to the right while maintaining the running GCD. If the GCD drops below k, further extensions cannot recover it, so we break early. This pruning keeps the average-case runtime low despite the quadratic worst-case bound.
